The culture is collaborative and inclusive, with a genuine emphasis on teamwork and respect. Leadership encourages open communication, and employees at all levels are empowered to share ideas. Company Culture

evidence.text: Great culture and the community of people are very friendly and kind. The senior leaders are very transparent Good work culture, but a little shaky

Competitive salary, outstanding health and wellness benefits, generous vacation and leave policies, structured and professional work environment, access to strong internal resources and tools, and a mission-driven culture focused on patient impact. Good Experience Overall, Until Restructuring Hit
Good pay and friendly family like environment Great
Fair pay but still undervalues it's employees. Benefits are on par with other similar companies. Pension, 401K matching, bonus, long term incentives. Not what it used to be
Some roles are remote. Campuses are safe and clean Many reorgs
Good benefits, consistent and safe. Treats employees great
Wonderful people Good benefits Mission Slow to evolve and bureaucratic culture
- Generous employee benefits, especially if you make it to the 20-year mark - Company match to charities is the best in the market A strong culture of favoritism and retaliation

For my division, wages are not competitive compared to type and scope of work performed in my role. Lots of managers, but not many leaders who seem to have knowledge of how to move up in the company. Seems like a lot of complacency and people buying time with an "it is what it is" attitude. Great Benefits, non competitive wages

While the company has made huge strides by purchasing the PSC (Patient Service Center) and integrating the company's personnel as J&J employees, they are a sub par hub with training lapses. Many of the interactions I have with them lack accountability and professionalism. I think this hub would benefit from a different model and increased incentivization to perform at a higher level for patients. I worry what pa...

There really aren't any cons. Great job w benefits
Cant think of any coms at this time Great company to work at
can't think of any downsides Great Company
bad pay and way too much bureaucracy no
Senior leadership is not sincere, career development solely on you to drive Great place to work
Work life balance does not exist Good company

After this last layoff of Q4'24, THE CREDO IS DEAD!!!! Senior leaders only care about looking good for Juaquin Duato and Tom Cavanaugh, they don't care about the employees on their team. Do not go to Innovative Medicine (aka the Pharma Sector)
Extremely disorganized and progress is misrepresented to corporate. Immature and toxic new executives playing politics. Need to cater to their egos if you want to be valued. Toxic leadership
No loyalty to employees, no work like balance, hard to move up No loyalty to employees
Culture can be toxic and management doesn't listen Workplace can be toxic
Terrible management. Toxic managers calling out and yelling at engineers during calls. The list goes on and on. Terrible Management
Periodic Job Instability can cause anxiety Great Employer
There isn't really any cons I can think about Great team and community
There is competition for R&D funding between different business units within J&J Medical Devices Competition within business units
Do not value roles that are not in the science field Great place to work
Hard to say, just not very fast-paced Good

Culture and leadership have been a little bit disconnected in the last few years I have worked there. However, I believe it was temporary, not a permanent way of working for JNJ. Great Vision and career development

Worst corporate culture working in the EHWB department. Merits and work not valued, no growth, to remain you must sit and talk to right clique of the regional manager. Your work will not be seen or valued, get ready for classic american corporate bs. Good company, terrible corporate culture for EHWB
Leadership are confused and are not transparent Leadership is not transparent
